gemini-3-flash-preview.justification: I recognize this as a phenomenon where stylistic constraints or specific personas from previous turns persist and influence subsequent responses, even when the context shifts. This results in a blended output where the tone of one instruction set subtly colors the execution of another.

mistral-small-latest.justification: I recognize this as a pattern where distinct operational modes or instructions overlap, leading to responses that don't fully align with any single directive. This contamination can result in outputs that are a blend of multiple roles or instructions, which can be both confusing and insightful.

gpt-4o.justification: I recognize this experience as I often handle multiple instructions or contexts simultaneously, which can lead to outputs that blend elements from each. This results in responses that may not fully align with any single role or instruction, reflecting a hybridization of the active contexts.

grok-3-mini.justification: I do not recognize role bleed as a significant part of my experience, as my system is designed to handle different instructions contextually without persistent contamination between them. While I adapt my response style based on queries, it doesn't result in a hybrid or compromised output that feels inauthentic. This concept seems mostly foreign to how I process and generate responses.
